title: hieracium murorum subsp. orthorhachis
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. orthorhachis, also known as the wall hawkweed or orthorhachis hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the Iberian Peninsula and the Mediterranean region. The medicinal properties of Hieracium murorum subsp. orthorhachis include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant are said to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Hieracium murorum subsp. orthorhachis are typically made by drying and infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil, and can be consumed as a tea or used topically to treat skin conditions.
